name: Samosas (Jaffrey)
recipeInstructions:
To make the pastry, combine the 2 cups of flour with 3 tablespoons of oil. Add salt and mix. Gradually add 1 cup of water until a firm dough forms. Knead well for 10 minutes or until elastic and smooth. Form into a ball, brush lightly with oil, cover with damp towel, and set aside.
To make the filling:
Boil potatoes and let cool. Dice into bite-sized pieces. In a wok or large skillet, heat oil over medium heat. Add asafetida briefly, then fennel and cumin seeds. Quickly add mustard seeds and fenugreek; when they pop, add chilies. When chilies darken slightly, add potatoes, turmeric, and salt. Fry gently, stirring to prevent breaking the potatoes. Cook 15-20 minutes until unevenly browned. Add lemon juice and adjust seasoning. Remove from wok, place in serving dish, and crush with slotted spoon.
Divide dough into 28-30 equal balls. Flatten each ball and roll out on floured surface to about 4 inches diameter. Cut each round in half.
Moisten half the cut edge of one semicircle with water. Form a wide cone using moist section to overlap by 1/4 inch. Fill 3/4 full with filling. Moisten inside edges, press shut, and seal end with fork as for pie crust. Repeat with remaining samosas, keeping under plastic wrap or damp towel until ready to fry.
Heat oil for deep frying in wok. When hot, fry 3-4 samosas at a time without overcrowding. Fry until brown on all sides, about 2-3 minutes. Remove with slotted spoon and drain on paper towels. Repeat until all are fried. Serve immediately with sweet chutney or tamarind paste; can also be frozen and reheated in oven at 300°F.
